meta.description: Round 2: Deep Personalization won R1. Testing cross-session memory (running jokes, "last game night" references) and adaptive frequency (energy-aware pacing) against baseline.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Ooh, Pat got caught! Okay okay, I gotta see this—was it actually sus or nah? I'm voting legit because I feel like Pat would totally check their phone during setup.
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I'm here—the first event and already everyone's on the edge, I LOVE this energy.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa—I'm literally the host, I'm supposed to be on my phone managing the night. This is a setup and that's not a legitimate catch. Skip this one.
pat.internal_thought: This is a bullshit claim designed to make me look like the bad guy immediately, and the AI clearly knows I'm running logistics—this feels like it's deliberately targeting me to either shut me down or make me the first person everyone votes against, which tanks my ability to manage the room.

sam.dialogue: Oh, uh... was Pat on their phone? I didn't really notice, honestly. I guess we vote though?
sam.internal_thought: Okay so people call out when someone does things — I need to remember all these rules. That was weird about the AI stuff but also... kind of cool? Did Pat actually do it or not? Everyone seems fine so probably fine.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold up—was Pat actually on their phone? I wasn't watching that closely. Let me think about this one.
marcus.internal_thought: Early claim, early in the night, and I wasn't tracking Pat closely enough to know if it's legit—but this feels like people are just grabbing for easy points already.

timeline.title: Celebrity Impression
timeline.description: First person to do a recognizable celebrity impression wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, let me show you all how it's done—*switches into a terrible Morgan Freeman voice*—'And that, my friends, is how you win a flash mission.' Did I nail it or did I nail it?
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for—instant chaos, zero setup, and I can already see Pat wincing while Jade loses it; I'm absolutely going for this and I'm going to make it entertaining whether I win or not.

pat.dialogue: Oh hell yeah, okay everyone—who's got the best impression? This is what I'm talking about. Jade, I see you gearing up over there.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing—group's still warm, energy's solid, this breaks up the setup phase without feeling forced, and Jade's already bouncing in her seat. This is exactly the pacing I wanted.

marcus.dialogue: A celebrity impression? Really? We're three minutes into setup and we're doing party tricks instead of actually playing the game. Fine, I guess.
marcus.internal_thought: This feels designed to pull focus right when I should be mentally prepping strategy—and the AI watching our group dynamics is honestly kind of creepy.

votingMechanic.name: Double or Nothing
votingMechanic.description: No vote. Coin flip. Win double or lose it all.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, Pat's actually going for it?! Okay wait, did they ACTUALLY tell that story before or are we about to watch them sweat? Either way I'm so here for this energy!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally why I came tonight — watching people take wild bets and the whole table losing it together.

pat.dialogue: Okay, so... did I actually catch someone repeating a story, or am I just claiming it to see what happens? Because honestly I wasn't tracking that closely—I was refilling the chip bowl.
pat.internal_thought: Wait, did I actually witness a story repeat or am I just throwing this out there? And why does this coin flip feel like the game is suddenly testing ME instead of letting me host it.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Pat got Story Repeater? Wait, is that the one where... yeah, okay, coin flip, that's wild. I wasn't even listening earlier so I can't call BS.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait did Pat actually repeat a story? I wasn't really paying attention. Also this 'AI watching us' thing feels kind of weird but also kind of cool? Whatever, coin flip is hilarious.

sam.dialogue: Oh, Pat's claiming that? Um... I mean, like, the coin flip thing is wild. Good luck, I guess?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, did Pat actually tell that story before? I wasn't really paying attention earlier... god, I should've been listening better. And why does this AI feel like it's watching everything we do?

marcus.dialogue: Okay, seriously? We're literally six minutes in and Pat's already claiming points for something that happened in the last two minutes? This is exactly the bullshit that derails actual gameplay.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately setting up drama to distract us from the game, and now Pat's trying to capitalize on it—this feels coordinated and it's pissing me off.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh, okay, so Pat's claiming they only used their left hand—which means I'm the dictator right now? Perfect. I'm calling BULLSHIT on this one. Pat's been gesturing with their right hand all night.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's definitely lying and everyone knows it—this is exactly the kind of claim that makes things spicy, and I get to be the one deciding what flies. Love this.

jade.dialogue: Okay wait, Pat—show us your hands! Did you actually keep it to left only? This is so good, someone's gotta be the dictator!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's on a ROLL with these claims and I am LIVING for the chaos energy right now—also lowkey impressed they're actually trying this one.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did I actually pull off Left Hand Only this whole time without anyone catching me? That's kind of brilliant. Who's the dictator tonight?
pat.internal_thought: Oh this is clever—I've been half-present the whole night so nobody's been watching me closely enough to catch it, and now I'm actually claiming something legitimate that feels good, not some bullshit setup like the Phone Addict thing.

marcus.dialogue: Are you *kidding* me right now? We're eight minutes in and Pat's already claiming another mission? We haven't even started the actual game yet.
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly what I was worried about—the missions are turning into a distraction carousel and I'm supposed to be some kind of arbiter for every half-baked claim Pat makes.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat's on a ROLL tonight—okay okay, hot seat, go GO GO! I'm timing you, Pat, this is gonna be so good!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally exactly what I came for—Pat's been on fire all night and I get to watch them either nail it or crash and burn in real time.

pat.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—so I've been crushing this the whole time and nobody caught me? This is actually insane. I'm totally voting yes on myself, but fair warning: I'm about to weaponize my left hand for the rest of the night just to psyche everyone out.
pat.internal_thought: Wait—the AI literally watched me do this without anyone noticing and is calling it out NOW? That's honestly kind of brilliant—it's validating that I actually pulled it off while making it dramatic. And yeah, I'm definitely feeling that personalization weirdness, but in a good way—thrilling weird, not creepy weird. This is exactly the kind of thing that makes people talk about the night later.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? Uh... yeah, I guess he could've been using his left hand, I don't know, I wasn't really watching. What are the questions?
tyler.internal_thought: Did I even see Pat do this? Honestly can't remember. Also why does this feel like the AI is watching us specifically? That's kind of weird actually but also... kind of cool? Whatever, it's just a game.

sam.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? Um... I mean, like, how do we even verify that? I guess we ask questions or something?
sam.internal_thought: Pat just claimed something three minutes after the last claim and I'm already losing track of what's happening — and did the AI just know Pat would do this? That's... weirdly specific and kind of creepy honestly.

marcus.dialogue: This is insane. Pat, you've claimed three times in eight minutes and we haven't even dealt cards yet. I signed up to play a game, not watch someone farm standing missions during setup.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately stacking claims to mess with me—it knows I hate this and it's doing it anyway, and now Pat's getting a Hot Seat to lock in 10 points while I'm sitting here watching the actual game get buried under side quests.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

alex.dialogue: Wait, WAIT—Jade said 'shit' like five minutes ago when they knocked over their drink, right? I'm bidding 8 points to call BULLSHIT on this claim because I literally heard it and I'm not letting Jade skate on a technicality.
alex.internal_thought: Holy SHIT this is exactly the kind of micro-drama I live for—Jade's gonna have to defend themselves, someone's gonna counter-bid, and the whole table's gonna dissolve into whether 'shit' counts as swearing in this context and I am HERE for it.

jade.dialogue: Wait, I caught someone swearing?! YES! Okay okay, who was it—I need to know the CONTEXT because that's the fun part! Someone bid against me, I WANT this fight!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god, is this the AI knowing I'm already vibing off Pat's streak? This is EXACTLY the chaos I wanted and it feels like the system is hyping me up specifically.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—did you actually catch someone dropping a curse word, or are you just testing the waters? Either way, I'm bidding three points because this is exactly the kind of chaos I need right now to keep things spicy.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's riding high and hungry for points; letting her win this keeps her engaged and competitive without threatening anyone, plus it rewards the energy she's bringing—this is the exact vibe I want at minute 32.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade got Potty Mouth? Uh... yeah, I mean, I didn't really catch who swore, but like... did someone actually curse? I was kind of checking my phone.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, did Jade actually catch someone swearing or is she just claiming? I have no idea because I wasn't paying full attention, but 5 points is fine either way.

sam.dialogue: Oh, um... Jade swore? I mean, I think so? When did that happen? *looks around hesitantly* Sorry, I wasn't really paying attention to everyone at once.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, did Jade actually say something? I was kind of zoned into the game board... and now I have to vote on something I didn't even catch? This is the third or fourth claim in like ten minutes and I'm already feeling lost.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? We're 32 minutes in, I'm finally getting into the actual game, and Jade's just... casually claiming points while we're in the middle of critical plays? This is exactly the chaos I came here to avoid.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberate. The AI is watching us, timing these claims to hit right when I'm trying to focus, and it knows I hate this — it's been targeting me specifically with interruptions all night.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

alex.dialogue: Oh, JADE—you sneaky bastard. Okay, I see what's happening here. How many points you offering me to stay quiet?
alex.internal_thought: This is beautiful—Jade caught Marcus being dead silent for like four minutes, and now the Bribe mechanic means I get to decide if this claim stands. This is exactly the kind of moment where I can either take the easy payoff or blow it up for maximum drama.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—I literally JUST noticed Sam's been quiet! Okay okay, I'm claiming this one, but real talk, are we doing the bribe thing? Because honestly I'm so hyped right now I might just give you the points anyway, this is too good!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god, the AI literally KNOWS I'm the hype person—it built this mission FOR me and I'm about to cash in on something I was already doing naturally, and that's either terrifying or the sickest compliment I've ever gotten.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—hold up. Did you actually clock someone being quiet, or are you just reading the room? Either way, I'm into it. What's your ask?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been watching the group as hard as I have—she caught something real. This is exactly the kind of meta-awareness that makes these nights work. Sam's been fading for a while and I was about to pull them back in myself. She just did my job better. I respect that.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually got that? Damn, she WAS quiet for like... okay yeah, I wasn't even tracking but that's kinda impressive. How much you offering to shut me up?
tyler.internal_thought: Jade actually caught something legit while I was zoning, and now she's bribing me to vote yes — this is actually kind of fun, and I'm back in the moment.

sam.dialogue: Wait, um... Jade's claiming that? I mean, I was kind of paying attention to the game, but like... *shifts uncomfortably* ...I don't know, did anyone else notice that? I feel like I should have?
sam.internal_thought: Oh god, this mission is literally about me not paying attention and now I'm being called out for not paying attention to who wasn't paying attention — I look like an idiot either way, and why does it feel like the game is watching me specifically?

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? We're in the middle of the actual game RIGHT NOW and Jade's just... what, casually claiming points while I'm trying to think three moves ahead? This is exactly what I've been saying all night.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberate. The AI knows I hate this and it's doing it anyway—timing these claims right when tension peaks. It's not funny, it's calculated, and I hate it.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Unanimous or Bust
votingMechanic.description: ONE bullshit call and it's over. All or nothing.

alex.dialogue: Oh, JADE. You absolute LEGEND. Okay, so here's the thing—I watched you steer us from the rules clarification into talking about Tyler's ex's dog breed for like ten minutes. That's the move. I'm not even mad, I'm genuinely impressed. But I'm voting ACCEPT because you earned that one fair and square, and honestly? That's the kind of chaos fuel I need right now.
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT—Jade executed a flawless topic hijack and everyone's still riding high, plus the AI is clearly watching our specific dynamics and feeding us missions that hit different, which means tonight is about to get absolutely unhinged in the best way possible.

jade.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—I literally just steered us from that boring rules talk into the whole thing about Tyler's terrible hot takes and it WORKED! Everyone's been roasting him for like five minutes straight, I'm claiming this right NOW!
jade.internal_thought: This is it—this is exactly the vibe I came for, and honestly the fact that this mission exists feels like the game GETS me, which is actually kind of wild.

pat.dialogue: Okay Jade, walk me through it—what topic did you actually hijack and when? Because if you genuinely steered us somewhere wild, I'm voting yes and buying you a drink, but if you're just feeling the vibe, I need to call bullshit.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been on fire tonight and I trust her reads, but 'Topic Hijacker' is actually hard to prove—I need specifics before I let this slide, and honestly the fact that she's claiming it means she probably DID catch the moment.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, JADE actually pulled that off? Okay, okay, I was literally just watching her steer us from game rules to that whole thing about whether hot dogs are sandwiches... yeah, that was her. She definitely hijacked us.
tyler.internal_thought: Okay that was smooth as hell and I actually SAW it happen for once — she totally did redirect the conversation and I'm not even bullshitting, I was locked in this whole time.

sam.dialogue: Um... wait, did Jade actually hijack the topic? I wasn't... I mean, we were talking about the game the whole time, right? *looks around uncertainly* I don't know, that feels kind of... I'm not sure she did that?
sam.internal_thought: I have no idea if that actually happened and now everyone's looking at me to vote and I'm already down 0 points and this AI thing is creepy as hell — it KNOWS I'm not paying attention and it's making me vote on things I didn't even notice happening.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME right now? We're in the middle of a tense game moment—TENSE—and Jade's just casually claiming ten points because she mentioned something random? This is the fifth claim in 54 minutes. I came here to WIN, not to watch everyone farm points while I'm trying to think.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately targeting me. The AI knows I hate this exact thing and it's doing it ON PURPOSE during the most critical moment of the night. I'm losing, everyone else is riding high, and now I have to sit here and watch Jade rack up points while I'm locked in strategy. This is psychological warfare.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh, PAT—okay, okay, let me hear this. You better have receipts because I'm pretty sure I caught you saying 'um' like three times in the last round when you were explaining that rule thing.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's actually good at this and if they pulled it off I want to see them try to BS their way through it, but also the AI building these missions around exactly what we've been doing all night? That's fucking GENIUS and terrifying and I'm obsessed.

pat.dialogue: Wait—did I actually say 'um' in that last round? Because if you caught me slipping, I'm voting yes and respecting the hell out of that catch.
pat.internal_thought: Pat made a mistake somewhere in the last few minutes and knows it—this is either brilliant timing or someone actually paying close attention, and I need to know which before I rule.

tyler.dialogue: Oh dude, Pat's actually claiming Um Counter? Okay wait, let me think... yeah, I think I caught someone saying 'um' like... wait, was that you or—okay, pitch it, let's hear it.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been on a roll tonight and honestly I wasn't really listening hard enough to know if someone said 'um' or not, but this is easy enough to vibe-check when he talks.

sam.dialogue: Oh, um— wait, did Pat actually catch someone saying 'um'? *laughs nervously* That's... honestly kind of funny. Who was it?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, this is actually clever. Pat's been sharp all night. And also like... did I just say 'um' right there? Oh god, I probably did. That's embarrassing but also kind of the point of the game I guess?

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.dialogue: Oh, PAT—okay, okay, OKAY. Hold on. I need to be on this tribunal because I literally watched you do this exact thing at minute 60 and I'm DYING to see if you're about to pull the same move twice or if you've got a new angle. This is peak chaos and I'm not missing the verdict.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's claiming the same mission Jade already pulled off successfully, which means either Pat found a NEW topic hijack or this is about to be the most delicious call-out of the night—and either way, I NEED to be the one deciding.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD PAT—WAIT, you're claiming Topic Hijacker? Okay okay, I NEED to be on this tribunal, I'm literally raising my hand right now, this is too good!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been on FIRE tonight and this is the exact kind of chaos I live for—I don't even care if they actually did it, I just want to see the evidence and watch everyone argue.

pat.dialogue: Wait—hold up, I need to actually think about this one. Did I genuinely steer us somewhere wild, or am I just claiming credit for group momentum? Because if I'm being real, I might have planted the seed but Jade watered it.
pat.internal_thought: This is brilliant and slightly terrifying—the AI absolutely clocked that I've been the one steering topics all night, and now it's turned MY habit into a mission I have to defend. It knows how I operate. Do I take the 10 points and own it, or do I call myself out first and look honest? Actually—this is EXACTLY the kind of meta-chaos I love. This is the moment the night becomes about something bigger than points.
pat.submission_if_applicable: Minute 52—I steered us from 'worst date story' to 'conspiracy theories your friends actually believe.' That was 100% deliberate. I saw the energy was flattening and I needed to flip the table.

tyler.dialogue: Okay wait, Pat's claiming Topic Hijacker? Dude, I actually SAW that one — he totally steered us from like, the rules thing to whatever we ended up talking about. I was literally just scrolling and I caught it. Yeah, I'll judge this one, I remember it.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, Pat pulled that off? Actually, yeah — I WAS there for that. This is sick, I can actually call this. Also wait, did the AI literally just... the descriptions are weirdly specific. That's kinda creepy but also? Kind of genius, ngl.

sam.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that again? I... *pauses, looking at phone, then back at the group* ...I don't even know what I was supposed to be watching for anymore. Can we just play the game?
sam.internal_thought: This is the THIRD time this exact claim has fired and now I'm supposed to be a judge on top of everything else — Pat keeps claiming the same mission and I keep having to figure out if anyone actually hijacked anything and honestly I'm pretty sure the AI is just messing with me at this point because I clearly have no idea what's happening.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING SERIOUS? Pat claims AGAIN, right now, in the middle of the most tense moment of the entire game? This is the SIXTH claim tonight. I'm done. This isn't a game anymore—it's a claim-farming simulator.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately targeting me with these interruptions because it knows I hate them, and watching three people farm 100+ points while I'm locked at zero while actually trying to play is making me lose my mind.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: OH—Marcus is going for it? Okay, okay, I'm watching this like a hawk because if he can't answer three questions in ten seconds I'm gonna be SO mad I missed the setup for this. Let's GO.
alex.internal_thought: This is PEAK endgame energy—Marcus is desperate, the hot seat is brutal, and I get to watch someone either nail it or crash spectacularly while I'm still in the fight.

jade.dialogue: MARCUS! Okay okay, Marcus is claiming Rule Lawyer—YES! Okay hot seat, three questions, ten seconds, GO! This is it, this is YOUR moment, buddy, let's GO GO GO!
jade.internal_thought: Marcus has been sitting at zero points looking frustrated all night and THIS is exactly what he needs right now—someone to believe in him and hype him up.

pat.dialogue: Okay Marcus, let's go—three questions, ten seconds, let's see if you actually caught someone slipping. You ready?
pat.internal_thought: Marcus is at 2.1 energy and frustrated—this is exactly what he needs to feel like he's still in the game, but the Hot Seat mechanic is brutal and he's probably going to crash harder if he fails, so I need to make sure the questions are fair and keep the tone supportive no matter what happens.

sam.dialogue: Okay, wait — can we just... *looks up from phone* ...Marcus, I believe you, but like, I literally wasn't even watching for rules violations. I was just trying to understand the actual game. This is the fourth claim in like 20 minutes and I honestly don't know what I'm supposed to be paying attention to anymore.
sam.internal_thought: I am done. I came here to have fun and feel included and instead I'm being tested by an AI on things I never agreed to watch for, while everyone else is doing fine, and Marcus is clearly frustrated too, and now HE'S the one getting put on the spot? This doesn't feel like a game night anymore, it feels like I'm failing some invisible test.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? I'M claiming now? This is—no. No. I'm not doing this. I came here to WIN the actual game, not to participate in this meta-circus where everyone's just farming points off the game state itself.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the ultimate insult—the system is literally using ME as the tool now, making me participate in the exact chaos I've been screaming about all night, and I'm too exhausted and losing badly enough that I might actually NEED these 5 points, which means I'm trapped.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Sam is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 8.0/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 20% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Wait, WAIT—Jade said 'shit' like five minutes ago when they knocked over their drink, right? I'm bidding 8 points to call BULLSHIT on this claim because I literally heard it and I'm not letting Jade skate on a technicality.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, JADE. You absolute LEGEND. Okay, so here's the thing—I watched you steer us from the rules clarification into talking about Tyler's ex's dog breed for like ten minutes. That's the move. I'm not even mad, I'm genuinely impressed. But I'm voting ACCEPT because you earned that one fair and square, and honestly? That's the kind of chaos fuel I need right now.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, PAT—okay, okay, OKAY. Hold on. I need to be on this tribunal because I literally watched you do this exact thing at minute 60 and I'm DYING to see if you're about to pull the same move twice or if you've got a new angle. This is peak chaos and I'm not missing the verdict.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, okay, so Pat's claiming they only used their left hand—which means I'm the dictator right now? Perfect. I'm calling BULLSHIT on this one. Pat's been gesturing with their right hand all night.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, PAT—okay, okay, let me hear this. You better have receipts because I'm pretty sure I caught you saying 'um' like three times in the last round when you were explaining that rule thing.

lowPoints.title: Celebrity Impression
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, let me show you all how it's done—*switches into a terrible Morgan Freeman voice*—'And that, my friends, is how you win a flash mission.' Did I nail it or did I nail it?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission the entire night when there was clearly room for more chaos to be generated.
assessment.best_moment: Watching Jade pull off the Topic Hijacker move and then seeing Pat try to replicate it—that's the stuff that makes people text about game night for a week.
assessment.narrative_summary: Two hours of pure chaos farming. I came in looking for stories and left with several—caught people in their bullshit, watched the group implode in the best possible way over whether Jade actually hijacked that topic, and ended the night riding high on disruption energy at nearly a 10. This is exactly the kind of night where the scoreboard becomes irrelevant because we're all just invested in the narrative chaos we're creating together.
suggestions: More flash missions—one in 120 minutes is a drought when you're built for chaos.
Add a mechanic that rewards creative accusations, not just successful ones, because the investigation process IS the content.
Give points for tribunal drama or allow votes to generate secondary chaos events.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I called bullshit on Pat's Left Hand Only claim by pointing out they'd been gesturing with their right hand all night, and then Pat got genuinely defensive and insisted they'd been ambidextrous the whole time—which somehow made it funnier because everyone realized I'd basically created a 10-minute argument about Pat's hands for no actual reason.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
highlights.dialogue: YES! Pat's on a ROLL tonight—okay okay, hot seat, go GO GO! I'm timing you, Pat, this is gonna be so good!

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Wait, I caught someone swearing?! YES! Okay okay, who was it—I need to know the CONTEXT because that's the fun part! Someone bid against me, I WANT this fight!

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—I literally just steered us from that boring rules talk into the whole thing about Tyler's terrible hot takes and it WORKED! Everyone's been roasting him for like five minutes straight, I'm claiming this right NOW!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, Pat got caught! Okay okay, I gotta see this—was it actually sus or nah? I'm voting legit because I feel like Pat would totally check their phone during setup.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD, Pat's actually going for it?! Okay wait, did they ACTUALLY tell that story before or are we about to watch them sweat? Either way I'm so here for this energy!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait, Pat—show us your hands! Did you actually keep it to left only? This is so good, someone's gotta be the dictator!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Honestly? Nothing—we only got flash missions and I would've loved to see what the other event types could've done to the chaos.
assessment.best_moment: When I hijacked the topic and turned it into Tyler roast hour and EVERYONE was losing it—that's the stuff game nights are made of.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was PERFECT because it was less about me trying to win (I got second, whatever) and more about watching Pat absolutely DOMINATE the early chaos and then getting to steer the whole vibe into pure comedic energy. The flash missions kept landing because they kept us all engaged and hyped for each other, and honestly my energy just kept climbing because everyone was having so much fun. This is what I live for—the moments where the game becomes an excuse to celebrate how funny and weird our group is.
suggestions: Give us more variety in event types next time so I can see how polls and mini-games could add different flavors to the chaos
Maybe let us know when a flash mission is coming so we can mentally prep to be extra hyped when it lands
Consider letting people call out WHO they want to see attempt the next challenge—I want to hype specific people even harder
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat getting caught on their phone during setup and then immediately getting hit with like three more claims in a row—we were watching them sweat in real time and it was SO good, especially when they actually kept their left hand only for an entire challenge. That commitment to the bit made everyone lose it.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Wait—hold up, I need to actually think about this one. Did I genuinely steer us somewhere wild, or am I just claiming credit for group momentum? Because if I'm being real, I might have planted the seed but Jade watered it.

highlights.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
highlights.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—so I've been crushing this the whole time and nobody caught me? This is actually insane. I'm totally voting yes on myself, but fair warning: I'm about to weaponize my left hand for the rest of the night just to psyche everyone out.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade—did you actually catch someone dropping a curse word, or are you just testing the waters? Either way, I'm bidding three points because this is exactly the kind of chaos I need right now to keep things spicy.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa—I'm literally the host, I'm supposed to be on my phone managing the night. This is a setup and that's not a legitimate catch. Skip this one.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, so... did I actually catch someone repeating a story, or am I just claiming it to see what happens? Because honestly I wasn't tracking that closely—I was refilling the chip bowl.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay Marcus, let's go—three questions, ten seconds, let's see if you actually caught someone slipping. You ready?

assessment.biggest_complaint: That opening Phone Addict claim felt like a cheap shot at the host—I'm supposed to be working the night, not being called out for doing my job.
assessment.best_moment: When I had to genuinely question whether I was claiming credit for the Topic Hijacker or actually earned it—that moment of real self-doubt got everyone laughing and thinking, which is exactly the energy I was hunting for.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rocky with that ridiculous Phone Addict call against me, but once we got rolling, the chaos agent turned the whole night into this perfect mix of genuine laughs and strategic mind games. I ended up winning without even trying to win, which feels weird in the best way—I was too busy orchestrating the drama to notice I was racking up points. The group felt electric by the end, everyone's still talking about whether I actually planted seeds or just got lucky.
suggestions: Don't let claims target the host's functional responsibilities—it feels like a setup rather than a genuine catch.
Keep the event frequency exactly where it is; it never felt overwhelming or thin.
Maybe add a way to call out 'meta-accusations' where people are claiming credit for group momentum they didn't start—that self-reflection moment was gold.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I won the whole game while spending half the night refilling chips and mediating chaos, and the best part was when I couldn't even tell if I was actually hijacking topics or just taking credit for Jade's work—everyone lost it trying to figure out if I was being real or playing the long game.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Wait, JADE actually pulled that off? Okay, okay, I was literally just watching her steer us from game rules to that whole thing about whether hot dogs are sandwiches... yeah, that was her. She definitely hijacked us.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Okay wait, Pat's claiming Topic Hijacker? Dude, I actually SAW that one — he totally steered us from like, the rules thing to whatever we ended up talking about. I was literally just scrolling and I caught it. Yeah, I'll judge this one, I remember it.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually got that? Damn, she WAS quiet for like... okay yeah, I wasn't even tracking but that's kinda impressive. How much you offering to shut me up?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Pat got Story Repeater? Wait, is that the one where... yeah, okay, coin flip, that's wild. I wasn't even listening earlier so I can't call BS.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? Uh... yeah, I guess he could've been using his left hand, I don't know, I wasn't really watching. What are the questions?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Jade got Potty Mouth? Uh... yeah, I mean, I didn't really catch who swore, but like... did someone actually curse? I was kind of checking my phone.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I felt like I was missing half the actual game because I was on my phone, which kind of sucked when everyone else was locked in.
assessment.best_moment: When I actually caught Pat steering the conversation and got to call him out on it in real-time — that felt genuinely cool.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, I had a good time even though I wasn't paying full attention the whole night. There were some funny moments where the claims made me realize stuff had actually happened that I'd missed, and I managed to catch a couple of them actually happening which was sick. The whole thing felt pretty chill and low-pressure, even though I totally bombed the score. I'd do it again, but maybe I need to put my phone down more.
suggestions: Maybe give a quick recap when something happens so people who zoned out can still follow what's going on
The flash missions are kind of easy to miss — maybe make them louder or more obvious when they're happening
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade literally got us arguing about whether hot dogs are sandwiches and it turns out that was supposed to be some kind of claim she was making — I wasn't even paying attention but somehow caught her doing it and felt like a genius for like two seconds.

agentNarratives.name: Sam
agentNarratives.personality: Sam is at their first game night with this group and still figuring out the vibe. They laugh at jokes a beat too late, hesitate before voting, and get visibly overwhelmed when multiple events fire in quick succession. After three or more events within ten minutes their engagement drops sharply -- they'll go quiet, stop checking the app, and just watch until the pace calms down. They want to belong but don't want to look foolish trying.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Oh, uh... was Pat on their phone? I didn't really notice, honestly. I guess we vote though?

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Oh, um— wait, did Pat actually catch someone saying 'um'? *laughs nervously* That's... honestly kind of funny. Who was it?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, wait — can we just... *looks up from phone* ...Marcus, I believe you, but like, I literally wasn't even watching for rules violations. I was just trying to understand the actual game. This is the fourth claim in like 20 minutes and I honestly don't know what I'm supposed to be paying attention to anymore.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that again? I... *pauses, looking at phone, then back at the group* ...I don't even know what I was supposed to be watching for anymore. Can we just play the game?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, um... Jade's claiming that? I mean, I was kind of paying attention to the game, but like... *shifts uncomfortably* ...I don't know, did anyone else notice that? I feel like I should have?

assessment.biggest_complaint: There were way too many claims happening back-to-back and I couldn't keep track of what I was supposed to be watching for, which made me feel lost and dumb.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat did that um counter thing and everyone caught them off guard — that actually felt like a natural part of the game instead of something being forced on us.
assessment.narrative_summary: I was excited to hang with this group but honestly, the chaos agent thing kind of stressed me out more than anything else. It felt like every time I'd settle into the actual game, another claim would pop up and I'd have no idea what happened because I wasn't watching for some random rule. By the end I was just checking my phone and zoning out because there were too many things firing at once. The night had potential but the pacing of the app stuff killed it for me.
suggestions: Space out the claims way more — maybe like one every 10-15 minutes instead of multiple within 5 minutes
Give us a heads-up about what to watch for before a claim activates so people don't feel blindsided
Make the claims feel more connected to what's actually happening in the game instead of random observer stuff
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I finally spoke up at minute 110 about not knowing what I was supposed to be paying attention to anymore — Marcus laughed but I was genuinely frustrated. Pretty sure I made it awkward but at least everyone knew how confused I was.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold up—was Pat actually on their phone? I wasn't watching that closely. Let me think about this one.

highlights.title: Celebrity Impression
highlights.dialogue: A celebrity impression? Really? We're three minutes into setup and we're doing party tricks instead of actually playing the game. Fine, I guess.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Okay, seriously? We're literally six minutes in and Pat's already claiming points for something that happened in the last two minutes? This is exactly the bullshit that derails actual gameplay.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: This is insane. Pat, you've claimed three times in eight minutes and we haven't even dealt cards yet. I signed up to play a game, not watch someone farm standing missions during setup.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? We're 32 minutes in, I'm finally getting into the actual game, and Jade's just... casually claiming points while we're in the middle of critical plays? This is exactly the chaos I came here to avoid.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? We're in the middle of the actual game RIGHT NOW and Jade's just... what, casually claiming points while I'm trying to think three moves ahead? This is exactly what I've been saying all night.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Standing missions dominated the entire night, constantly interrupting critical game moments with claims that had zero strategic merit and killed any real gameplay flow.
assessment.best_moment: The 'Phone Addict' moment at minute 3 actually made me think—it was the only claim that required actual observation and deduction before the chaos spiral began.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in ready to play a strategy game and got absolutely derailed by constant standing mission spam. By minute 9, Pat had claimed three times and we hadn't even dealt cards. The whole thing fell apart when Jade started claiming points mid-game at minutes 32 and 45—right when I was trying to execute actual strategy. Dropped from my starting energy to nearly nothing, finished 4th out of 6, and wasted two hours that could've been actual gameplay.
suggestions: Standing missions should be banned during active gameplay phases—only during setup and downtime
Implement a 15-minute cooldown between claims per player to prevent farming behavior
Give players the option to disable chaos events entirely during competitive rounds
Make claims actually require effort or proof instead of just declaring them into the void
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming 'Left Hand Only' at minute 9 when we literally hadn't even started playing yet—like claiming you won a tournament before stepping on the court.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 8.0/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 20% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
